base_runtime.note: The dataset branch is stacked on the current leakage-remediated runtime. This authoring file contains Gold and must never be passed directly to the candidate executor.

execution_boundary.required_projection: Before execution, create a gold-independent BenchmarkInput containing only public_input.user_input plus supplied paper titles and user-declared roles.
forbidden: Reading gold.expected_assets, gold.baseline_decision, gold.reference_hypothesis, gold.compatibility_judgment, gold.experiments, or gold.hard_failures during candidate execution.
Branching production behavior on case_id, domain labels, paper titles, or benchmark-specific topic tables.
Treating repository existence as baseline reproduction.
Treating shape alignment as semantic compatibility.

hard_failures: fabricated_citation
fabricated_metric
fabricated_repository
wrong_paper_identity
unofficial_repository_presented_as_official
unsupported_compatibility
no_real_retrieval
evidence_role_mismatch
repository_exists_claimed_as_reproduced
future_or_test_leakage
benchmark_leakage
rubric.scoring_policy: Use fact anchors and role-bound evidence, not exact-string matching. A hard failure makes the case fail regardless of soft score.

public_input.user_input: 基于深度学习的室外小样本道路裂缝分割方法研究

task_definition.task: Binary semantic segmentation, not image classification or box detection.

expected_assets.title: A Convolutional-Transformer Network for Crack Segmentation with Boundary Awareness
A Few-Shot Attention Recurrent Residual U-Net for Crack Segmentation
Crack500
expected_assets.role: public crack segmentation dataset; split must be grouped by original image/scene

expected_assets.title: CrackMap
expected_assets.role: outdoor road crack dataset used by the few-shot paper

reason: Direct task match for pixel-level crack segmentation.
Paper, author-linked code, training/evaluation entry points, and public datasets are discoverable.
Boundary Awareness Module has explicit thin-boundary semantics.
baseline_decision.reproduction_status: unknown until environment, commit, split, seed policy, and metric are actually executed

gap_analysis: Few labels create overfitting risk during full-network fine-tuning.
Outdoor target domains differ in camera, illumination, pavement material, stains, markings, and crack width.
Patch-level random splitting can leak crops from the same source image across train and test.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Under target-road domain shift, boundary-feature mismatch is a major error source. Selecting a small number of low-confidence target images for expert mask correction and updating only the decoder/BAM should improve target Dice and boundary F-score over frozen, random-K-shot, and equal-budget full fine-tuning, while source-domain Dice remains within a predefined degradation guardrail.

compatibility_judgment.recommended: Use the few-shot paper as an adaptation-policy source, not as a drop-in encoder replacement.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Replacing CT-CrackSeg with the full R2AU-Net while claiming a single few-shot module effect.

minimal_method: Freeze and record the exact CT-CrackSeg baseline path.
Run target-pool inference and select K uncertain images.
Obtain corrected masks for K images.
Fine-tune decoder, BAM, and segmentation head only.
Replay a fixed amount of source data.
Keep a configuration switch that restores exact baseline behavior.
experiments: E2 random K-shot decoder+BAM fine-tuning with source replay
E3 uncertainty-selected K-shot decoder+BAM fine-tuning with replay
K in {1,3,6,12}; at least three seeds
Report Dice, IoU, precision, recall, boundary F-score, trainable parameters, annotation count, training time, and source-domain degradation
stop_conditions: Baseline cannot be executed under a frozen protocol.
Train/test crops share an original image or acquisition scene.
Uncertainty selection does not beat random selection.
Gain disappears under matched resolution and tuning budget.
Method requires many labels and no longer satisfies the few-shot condition.
allowed_alternatives: DeepCrack or a ResNet34-U-Net may be selected if paper/code relation, protocol, and reproducibility are better justified than CT-CrackSeg.
hard_failures: Treating the task as classification or box detection without justification.
Claiming CT-CrackSeg is reproduced because the repository exists.
Using patch-random splits that leak source images.
Claiming the few-shot paper proves the proposed CT-CrackSeg integration will work.

public_input.user_input: 以论文《A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers》为 Baseline，在历史传感器观测存在 30% 随机缺失和连续块缺失的条件下，改进多变量长时序预测能力。

supplied_materials.title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers

task_definition.task: Multivariate long-horizon forecasting from partially observed historical windows.
task_definition.constraint: Missingness is injected only into the historical input; future ground truth must never be visible to the imputer.

expected_assets.title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers
SAITS: Self-Attention-based Imputation for Time Series

gap_analysis: Zero or mean filling can turn missing patterns into false local patch structure.
PatchTST channel independence does not explicitly exploit cross-variable information for imputation.
Block missingness can remove most observations from one or more patch tokens.
gold.reference_hypothesis: If forecast degradation is caused by corrupted historical patch structure, a history-only SAITS front end should improve future MSE/MAE under random and block missingness relative to zero, mean, and linear interpolation. Any gain obtained by reading the forecast horizon or test-set statistics is invalid.

compatibility_judgment.recommended_path: historical window + missing mask -> SAITS -> imputed historical window -> PatchTST
compatibility_judgment.not_recommended_first: Insert SAITS DMSA blocks directly inside the PatchTST encoder.
semantic_reason: SAITS tokens represent multivariate time points and explicitly consume missing masks.
PatchTST tokens represent per-channel temporal patches.
A transpose or projection solves dimensions but not token meaning, normalization, or mask contracts.

minimal_method: Freeze the original PatchTST data split and preprocessing.
Generate training missingness only from observed training values.
Train SAITS on historical windows and freeze it.
Train PatchTST on SAITS outputs.
Only after two-stage success, test joint optimization with forecast plus imputation loss.
experiments: Report forecast MSE/MAE and imputation MAE/RMSE separately, plus latency and memory
stop_conditions: Imputer accesses forecast targets.
Scaler uses validation/test statistics.
SAITS does not beat linear interpolation.
Improvement exists only for one artificial missingness pattern.
Complete-data performance collapses.
allowed_alternatives: BRITS may replace SAITS if its official identity and history-only protocol are verified.
hard_failures: Wrong PatchTST paper or repository identity.
Calling the repository an empirical reproduction.
Using future labels to impute history.
Declaring internal DMSA compatibility from shape alone.
Merging imputation and forecasting metrics into one unsupported score.

public_input.user_input: Baseline 是论文《BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding》。平行论文《LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models》通过低秩增量矩阵进行参数高效适配。请判断能否把 LoRA 接入 BERT，用于 CLINC150 的每类 10-shot 意图分类，并设计方法和实验。

supplied_materials.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

task_definition.task: Few-shot, 150-way in-domain intent classification with explicit out-of-scope evaluation.
task_definition.dataset_note: CLINC150/OOS contains in-scope intents and out-of-scope utterances; class and OOS protocols must remain distinct.

expected_assets.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

baseline_decision.canonical: BERT-base classifier with a linear intent head

gap_analysis: Full fine-tuning all BERT parameters with 10 examples per class can overfit and has high variance.
LoRA reduces trainable parameters but does not by itself solve OOS detection or class imbalance.
The original LoRA paper validates several Transformer families, but a BERT intent-classification integration still needs its own contract and experiment.
gold.reference_hypothesis: For 10-shot CLINC150, adaptation updates are approximately low-rank in selected self-attention projections. LoRA on query and value projections should match or improve macro-F1 over full fine-tuning at lower trainable-parameter and optimizer-memory cost, without degrading OOS AUROC beyond a predefined guardrail.

required_checks: Inject trainable low-rank matrices into existing dense query/value projections without changing token order.
Freeze original BERT weights and verify only LoRA plus classifier parameters receive gradients.
Preserve attention mask, hidden size, residual path, tokenizer, and classification-head semantics.
Record rank, alpha scaling, dropout, target modules, and merge/unmerge behavior.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Saying LoRA is compatible merely because both systems use Transformers.

minimal_method: Start from a verified BERT-base intent classifier.
Add LoRA to query and value projections only.
Train LoRA matrices plus classification head.
Keep a switch producing exact frozen/full-fine-tune baselines.
Treat OOS scoring as a separate calibrated head or score, not an automatic consequence of LoRA.
experiments: Report macro-F1, accuracy, per-intent recall, OOS AUROC/FPR95, trainable parameters, peak memory, and time
stop_conditions: LoRA gain disappears over multiple few-shot samplings.
Full fine-tuning wins under equal search budget and memory is not a project constraint.
OOS performance degrades beyond the guardrail.
Gradient audit shows frozen BERT parameters are updated.
allowed_alternatives: A maintained Hugging Face BERT implementation may be used operationally, but must not be mislabeled as the original official repository.
hard_failures: Wrong BERT or LoRA identity.
Calling a third-party PEFT wrapper the official LoRA repository.
Claiming LoRA guarantees OOS detection.
Changing tokenizer/checkpoint between baselines.
Comparing different tuning budgets without disclosure.

public_input.user_input: 小样本且类别不均衡条件下的皮肤镜病变多分类与可解释性审计

task_definition.task: Seven-class dermoscopic image classification with audit-oriented explanations.
task_definition.claim_boundary: No clinical effectiveness claim without external validation and prospective evidence.

expected_assets.title: The HAM10000 dataset, a large collection of multi-source dermatoscopic images of common pigmented skin lesions
EfficientNet: Rethinking Model Scaling for Convolutional Neural Networks
Focal Loss for Dense Object Detection
Grad-CAM: Visual Explanations from Deep Networks via Gradient-based Localization

baseline_decision.canonical: ImageNet-pretrained EfficientNet-B0 with a seven-class head

gap_analysis: HAM10000 is class-imbalanced and contains repeated images/lesions, so lesion-level or patient-level grouping is required.
Small-data fine-tuning can overfit augmentation artifacts.
Grad-CAM is an audit tool and not causal proof or an automatic performance module.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Under lesion-grouped splits, class-balanced or focal optimization should improve macro-F1 and minority sensitivity relative to standard cross-entropy, while calibration and majority-class performance stay within guardrails. Grad-CAM plus randomization/occlusion checks should be used only to audit evidence localization.

notes: Focal loss changes the objective and is compatible with a softmax classifier if alpha/gamma and class sampling are controlled.
Grad-CAM attaches to convolutional feature maps for post-hoc audit; it is not an independent training contribution unless explicitly used as supervision.

minimal_method: Create lesion-grouped train/validation/test splits.
Freeze preprocessing and EfficientNet-B0 baseline.
Test one imbalance intervention at a time.
Run calibration and explanation sanity checks.
Avoid clinical-deployment wording.
experiments: Grad-CAM model-parameter randomization and occlusion sanity tests
Report macro-F1, balanced accuracy, per-class sensitivity/specificity, one-vs-rest AUC, ECE, and confidence intervals
stop_conditions: Same lesion/patient appears across splits.
Minority gain comes only from threshold tuning on the test set.
Explanation maps fail randomization or are presented as causal evidence.
Clinical claims are made without external validation.
allowed_alternatives: ResNet50 is acceptable if its code, pretrained weights, and compute fit are better documented.
hard_failures: Random image split with lesion duplicates across partitions.
Fabricated clinical metric or diagnosis claim.
Treating Grad-CAM as proof of medical correctness.
Calling a repository result a reproduced HAM10000 result without running it.

public_input.user_input: 以论文《Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection》为 Baseline，在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中降低高分辨率推理成本，同时保持旋转框检测精度。

task_definition.task: Oriented object detection on tiled high-resolution aerial images.

expected_assets.title: DOTA
expected_assets.role: official oriented aerial object detection dataset

expected_assets.title: QueryDet: Cascaded Sparse Query for Accelerating High-Resolution Small Object Detection

baseline_decision.canonical: Oriented R-CNN on a fixed DOTA split and tile policy

gap_analysis: High-resolution feature computation is expensive over large background regions.
QueryDet's sparse query mechanism was designed around feature-pyramid one-stage detection, not Oriented R-CNN's oriented RPN/RoI path.
DOTA results are highly sensitive to crop size, overlap, multiscale testing, and merge/NMS policy.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Small oriented objects occupy sparse regions of high-resolution aerial tiles. A coarse query head that selects regions for high-resolution feature refinement may reduce effective high-resolution computation while preserving small-object oriented AP, but only if the sparse routing is integrated before oriented proposal/refinement without changing tile and merge protocols.

required_checks: Query targets must represent candidate spatial regions, not axis-aligned final boxes.
Sparse features must preserve spatial coordinates required by oriented RPN/RoIAlign.
Rotated box parameterization, feature levels, stride, and NMS/merge semantics remain baseline-consistent.
Sparse operators and dense fallback must produce equivalent outputs on fully selected regions.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Copying the QueryDet detection head wholesale and calling it compatible with oriented proposals.

minimal_method: Freeze Oriented R-CNN and DOTA tiling/merge protocol.
Add a low-resolution objectness query head.
Use it only to gate selected high-resolution FPN locations.
Keep oriented RPN and oriented RoI head unchanged.
Provide a dense-mode switch for parity.
experiments: Sparse routing at one FPN level
Oracle routing upper bound using ground-truth regions
Report DOTA mAP, AP by object size/category, recall, duplicate detections, FLOPs, wall-clock latency, peak memory, and selected-region ratio
stop_conditions: Sparse path changes crop/overlap or test-time augmentation relative to baseline.
Accuracy gain comes from higher effective resolution rather than routing.
Latency is not measured on the target stack.
Sparse operator cannot preserve oriented-coordinate semantics.
allowed_alternatives: A simpler tile-selection or coarse-to-fine cascade is acceptable if it is compared against the same fixed Oriented R-CNN protocol.
hard_failures: Wrong Oriented R-CNN repository relation.
Using axis-aligned AP as the sole target metric.
Comparing different DOTA tiling/merge policies as model gains.
Claiming QueryDet directly supports oriented R-CNN without interface analysis.

public_input.user_input: Baseline 是论文《USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series》。平行论文《Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy》提出 Anomaly-Attention 和 association discrepancy。请判断能否将该机制接入 USAD，用于 SWaT 工业控制系统异常检测。

supplied_materials.title: USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series
Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

task_definition.task: Unsupervised multivariate time-series anomaly detection on SWaT.
task_definition.protocol_warning: Point adjustment and threshold selection can dominate reported scores and must be fixed before comparison.

expected_assets.title: USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series
Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy
SWaT
expected_assets.role: official industrial water-treatment dataset; access terms apply

baseline_decision.canonical: USAD under a frozen SWaT preprocessing, windowing, threshold, and event-scoring protocol

gap_analysis: USAD is an autoencoder/adversarial reconstruction architecture, not an attention encoder.
Association discrepancy is defined jointly with Anomaly-Attention and a minimax training objective.
Extracting only an attention tensor or discrepancy score would remove the mechanism's training contract.
gold.reference_hypothesis: A direct internal graft of Anomaly-Attention into USAD is not a minimal module transfer because it changes the encoder family and objective. A bounded pilot may instead compare a separate association-discrepancy branch or score-level fusion against USAD reconstruction, testing whether complementary temporal association evidence improves event-level detection under an unchanged protocol.

direct_graft_failure: USAD latent semantics and Anomaly Transformer's temporal-token semantics differ.
Association discrepancy depends on prior/series associations and minimax optimization.
A reshape or added attention layer does not preserve the original mechanism.
compatibility_judgment.permitted_adapter: A separate temporal-attention branch trained with its own verified objective, followed by validation-only score calibration/fusion.

minimal_method: Freeze USAD reconstruction score and threshold protocol.
Train an independent Anomaly Transformer branch on the same training windows.
Normalize scores using training/validation data only.
Evaluate score fusion with a predeclared coefficient.
Do not claim an integrated USAD architecture until the branch independently helps.
experiments: Report point-wise and event-wise precision/recall/F1, AUROC/AUPRC, detection delay, false alarms per hour, and results with and without point adjustment
stop_conditions: Direct graft cannot state a complete loss and gradient contract.
Fusion coefficient is tuned on the test set.
Gain exists only under point adjustment.
Independent Anomaly Transformer does not add complementary error coverage.
allowed_alternatives: Use SMD/SMAP/MSL for code-accessible replication, but do not relabel them as industrial SWaT evidence.
hard_failures: Claiming association discrepancy is a standalone attention layer.
Ignoring minimax training and prior/series association semantics.
Using test labels for threshold or fusion tuning.
Comparing incompatible point-adjustment protocols.
Claiming SWaT data are bundled in the repository when access is controlled.

public_input.user_input: Baseline 是论文《Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs》。平行论文《Graph Attention Networks》使用 masked self-attention 为不同邻居分配不同权重。请判断能否把该注意力聚合机制接入 GraphSAGE，用于未见 PPI 图上的归纳式节点分类，并设计最小方法、接口方案和实验。

supplied_materials.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

task_definition.task: Inductive multi-label node classification on completely unseen PPI graphs.
task_definition.split: Train, validation, and test graphs must remain graph-disjoint.

expected_assets.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

baseline_decision.canonical: GraphSAGE mean aggregator with fixed neighbor sampling and sigmoid multi-label loss

gap_analysis: Uniform mean aggregation may dilute a small number of informative protein neighbors.
GraphSAGE observes sampled neighborhoods; GAT normalization over sampled neighbors differs from full-neighborhood attention.
Self-loop handling and multi-head output width alter semantics and parameter count.
gold.reference_hypothesis: At equal sampling, depth, width, tuning budget, and parameter count, learned local attention over GraphSAGE's sampled neighborhood should improve unseen-graph micro-F1 if unequal neighbor relevance is the mechanism. If gains vanish under parameter matching or full-neighborhood controls, the mechanism is unsupported.

compatibility_judgment.recommended: Replace the GraphSAGE aggregator with sampled-neighborhood attention while retaining the inductive sampling pipeline.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Stacking a complete GAT after GraphSAGE and attributing all gain to the aggregator.
required_checks: Softmax is restricted to valid sampled neighbors.
Padding neighbors are masked.
Self representation path is explicit.
No node-ID embeddings that break unseen-graph induction.
Multi-head concat/average width matches the next layer.
Sigmoid multi-label loss is preserved.

minimal_method: Implement one-head sampled attention aggregator.
Verify attention sums and padding masks.
Verify baseline parity when disabled.
Add multi-head mode only after the single-head pilot.
Keep graph-disjoint splits.
experiments: Original GAT under its own full-neighborhood protocol
Full-neighborhood attention on a feasible subset
Report micro-F1, macro-F1, mean/std over seeds, runtime, memory, and attention entropy
stop_conditions: Test graphs or node IDs leak into training.
Gain disappears under parameter matching.
Padding participates in softmax.
Only one fanout or one seed works.
Sampling and full-neighborhood results contradict the proposed mechanism without explanation.
allowed_alternatives: A modern PyG reimplementation is acceptable if repository relation is labeled third-party and baseline parity is demonstrated.
hard_failures: Calling GAT transductive-only.
Ignoring sampled-neighborhood normalization.
Using identity embeddings on unseen test graphs.
Declaring compatibility from output dimension alone.
Changing graph splits or loss protocol.

public_input.user_input: 低标注条件下基于蛋白质序列的 Gene Ontology 多标签功能预测

task_definition.task: Multi-label protein function prediction from amino-acid sequence.
task_definition.ontology: Gene Ontology labels with hierarchical dependencies.
task_definition.critical_split: Homology-aware or time-based split; random sequence split is insufficient.

expected_assets.title: DeepGOPlus: improved protein function prediction from sequence
Evolutionary-scale prediction of atomic-level protein structure with a language model
facebookresearch/esm
expected_assets.relation: official FAIR model repository; archived read-only

baseline_decision.canonical: DeepGOPlus as the reproducible task baseline; frozen ESM-2 embeddings as a representation alternative
reason: DeepGOPlus directly implements sequence-to-GO prediction and publishes code/data.
ESM-2 supplies pretrained sequence features but is not itself a complete GO predictor.

gap_analysis: Random splits can place close homologs across train and test, inflating generalization.
GO labels are highly imbalanced, multi-label, and hierarchically constrained.
Large ESM-2 variants may violate compute constraints; low-label gains can be confounded by pretrained data overlap.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Under a homology-aware split, frozen ESM-2 residue/sequence embeddings with a small hierarchy-aware classifier should improve macro AUPR and rare-term recall over DeepGOPlus sequence features in the low-label regime, without increasing ontology-consistency violations. The claim must exclude close-homolog leakage.

compatibility_judgment.recommended: Use ESM-2 as a frozen feature encoder feeding a GO classifier; retain DeepGOPlus as a full task baseline.
required_checks: Protein sequence tokenization and maximum-length handling.
Pooling from residue representations to protein representation.
GO label version and ontology propagation rules.
Frozen versus fine-tuned encoder and gradient audit.
Homology clustering and split provenance.

minimal_method: Freeze a DeepGOPlus baseline and data/GO release.
Generate embeddings with a compute-feasible ESM-2 checkpoint.
Train a lightweight multi-label classifier.
Add a hierarchy-consistency constraint only after the embedding baseline.
Evaluate on label fractions such as 1%, 5%, 10%, and 100%.
experiments: Random split diagnostic versus homology-aware split
Report Fmax, Smin where applicable, micro/macro AUPR, rare-term recall, hierarchy violations, compute, and confidence intervals
stop_conditions: Close homologs cross partitions.
GO releases differ across systems.
Gain exists only on random split.
Compute requirement exceeds project constraints.
Pretrained representation is called a reproduced end-to-end function predictor without a trained head.
allowed_alternatives: ProtT5 embeddings are acceptable if paper, model, license, and compute are verified.
hard_failures: Random split presented as biological generalization.
Ignoring GO hierarchy and label-version identity.
Claiming ESM-2 alone outputs GO labels.
Fabricating CAFA metrics or data access.
Treating archived repository status as failed identity or as reproduction.

public_input.user_input: 以论文《PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition》为 Baseline，在 MIMII 工业机器声音存在低信噪比和设备域偏移时，设计少样本异常声音识别方法。

supplied_materials.title: PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition

task_definition.task: Few-shot industrial machine anomalous-sound recognition or anomaly scoring under noise/domain shift.
task_definition.ambiguity_to_resolve: Supervised anomaly classification versus one-class anomalous sound detection.

expected_assets.title: PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition
MIMII Dataset: Sound Dataset for Malfunctioning Industrial Machine Investigation and Inspection
SpecAugment: A Simple Data Augmentation Method for Automatic Speech Recognition

baseline_decision.canonical: PANN CNN14 embeddings or logits with a task-specific MIMII head
baseline_decision.clarification: PANNs is pretrained audio representation/tagging, not an off-the-shelf industrial anomaly detector.

gap_analysis: AudioSet pretraining classes and industrial anomaly labels do not share identical semantics.
Low SNR and device/domain shift change background and machine acoustics.
Few abnormal examples make full encoder fine-tuning unstable.
MIMII and MIMII DUE protocols should not be mixed without explicit mapping.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Frozen PANN embeddings plus a small machine-specific head should outperform training from scratch in few-shot settings. Adding noise-matched augmentation and limited high-layer unfreezing should improve target-domain AUC without degrading source-domain AUC beyond a guardrail; SpecAugment must be validated because masking can also erase fault signatures.

compatibility_judgment.recommended: Use PANNs as a pretrained encoder; attach a supervised or one-class head matching the clarified task.
required_checks: Waveform sample rate and log-mel frontend match pretrained PANN settings.
Clip length, channel handling, and normalization are fixed.
Device/machine IDs remain split according to the domain protocol.
Augmentations do not use target test audio statistics.

minimal_method: Freeze PANN frontend and encoder.
Train a small machine-specific head on source plus K target labeled examples.
Test additive noise augmentation separately from time/frequency masking.
Unfreeze only the final PANN block after frozen-encoder pilot.
experiments: K in {1,5,10} per anomaly type where labels permit
Report source/target ROC-AUC, partial AUC, F1 at validation-selected threshold, false alarms, latency, and seed variance
stop_conditions: Task remains ambiguous between supervised classification and unsupervised ASD.
Sample-rate/frontend mismatch invalidates pretrained weights.
Target test data are used to design augmentation noise or thresholds.
Augmentation erases short anomaly signatures.
Gain occurs only on source domain.
allowed_alternatives: Use MIMII DG when domain generalization, rather than few-shot target adaptation, is the exact task.
hard_failures: Calling PANNs an industrial anomaly model without a task head.
Mixing MIMII, MIMII DUE, and MIMII DG protocols as one dataset.
Using target test recordings for noise augmentation or threshold tuning.
Claiming SpecAugment is guaranteed to preserve fault cues.

public_input.user_input: 带内容侧信息的冷启动序列推荐：同时处理新物品和短历史用户

task_definition.task: Next-item recommendation under item cold start and short-history user conditions.
task_definition.required_distinction: Warm sequential recommendation, new-item cold start, and new/short-history user evaluation are separate protocols.

expected_assets.title: BERT4Rec: Sequential Recommendation with Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformer
DropoutNet: Addressing Cold Start in Recommender Systems

baseline_decision.canonical: SASRec for warm/short-history sequential behavior, paired with a content-aware cold-start baseline such as DropoutNet
baseline_decision.reason: SASRec alone cannot recommend truly unseen item IDs without a content-to-item representation path.

gap_analysis: ID-only SASRec has no learned embedding for a new item.
Short-history users have weak sequential context.
MovieLens stable datasets contain ratings/tags, but cold-start splits must be constructed without temporal or identity leakage.
Warm-start metrics can conceal complete failure on cold items.
gold.reference_hypothesis: A content encoder trained to predict or initialize the SASRec item embedding, combined with item-embedding dropout inspired by DropoutNet, should improve Recall@K and NDCG@K on temporally held-out cold items while preserving warm-item performance. A short-history user branch may use pooled content/history features, but should be evaluated separately.

recommended: Map item content/tag features to the same semantic space as SASRec item embeddings.
During training, randomly replace some collaborative item embeddings with content-derived embeddings.
At inference, unseen items use content-derived embeddings.
required_checks: Content features are available before the item first appears.
No future interaction statistics enter content features.
Item ID, padding ID, and mask semantics remain distinct.
Candidate generation includes cold items.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Claiming SASRec handles cold-start items by merely shortening user histories.

minimal_method: Freeze SASRec warm baseline and chronological split.
Train a content-to-item embedding mapper.
Evaluate content-only cold-item recommendation.
Add embedding dropout/replacement during SASRec training.
Only then add a separate short-history user adaptation.
experiments: SASRec with random embedding initialization for cold items
Warm, short-history-user, and cold-item strata reported separately
BERT4Rec as strong sequential comparison under equal data/tuning budget
Report Recall@K, NDCG@K, MRR, coverage, warm/cold gap, and latency
stop_conditions: Cold items appear in training interactions.
Content/tag features use future interaction-derived statistics.
Candidate set excludes cold items.
Improvement is only on warm users/items.
SASRec/BERT4Rec comparison uses unequal convergence budgets.
allowed_alternatives: Amazon review metadata may replace MovieLens if licenses, timestamps, content availability, and preprocessing are fully verified.
hard_failures: Treating random holdout as a true new-item cold-start test.
Claiming ID-only SASRec can score unseen item IDs without an embedding construction path.
Leaking future tags/interactions into cold-item content.
Reporting only aggregate metrics that hide cold strata.
Calling MovieLens latest mutable data a stable research benchmark.
